nuggets.headline: Jamee Cornelia's first creative venture was a clothing company for a skate team
nuggets.text: Before becoming a musician, Jamee Cornelia started in the creative industry at just 15, launching her own clothing company. The original goal? To sponsor the skate team she was a part of, showcasing her entrepreneurial spirit from an early age. This early foray into multimedia even led her to become the team's videographer, using cameras from her high school AFJROTC program.

source.title: Conversations with the Inspiring Jamee Cornelia
source.quoteSnippet: I got into the creative industry at 15 with my own clothing company which was created to sponsor a skate team I was in.

nuggets.headline: Jamee Cornelia navigated a male-dominated industry as a queer Black woman
nuggets.text: Jamee Cornelia has openly spoken about the significant challenges she's faced as a Queer Black Woman in the South's male-dominated music industry. Early in her career, she found it difficult to be taken seriously and secure fair payment, especially in areas like Cobb County, due to her youth, gender, race, and sexual orientation. Her resilience led her to flourish in East Atlanta, a more welcoming environment.

source.title: Conversations with the Inspiring Jamee Cornelia
source.quoteSnippet: Well, the biggest bump for me is that I am a Queer Black Woman in a male-dominated field in the South. When I started, it was hard for me to get new people to want to work with me because I wasn’t taken seriously due to my youth gender race and at times sexual orientation.

nuggets.headline: Explore Nappy Nina if you're into Jamee Cornelia's independent hip-hop scene
nuggets.text: If you're digging into Jamee Cornelia's world of independent hip-hop, especially since she co-runs her own indie label L7 Records, you'll definitely want to check out Nappy Nina. These artists share a similar space within the vibrant, independent hip-hop scene, showcasing fresh voices outside mainstream constraints.

source.title: Conversations with the Inspiring Jamee Cornelia
source.quoteSnippet: Currently, I still release solo music under Jamee Cornelia as well as direct music videos and me and the friends of the group I use to be in called Visibly Inflight run our own indie label “L7 Records”

artistSummary: Jamee Cornelia is a multifaceted artist from Marietta, GA, whose creative journey started with fashion and videography before leading her to lead a punk band and forge a distinctive path in hip-hop. She's also a director and co-runs the independent label L7 Records.
